note_evidence: Mrs. Hilpert is a 76-year-old woman admitted to Maple Grove for rehabilitation following approximately one week of hospitalization.
rationale: Age, admission for rehab, and ~1 week hospitalization all documented.

note_evidence: significant deconditioning, describing profound fatigue and functional decline in her legs to the point of being unable to ambulate
rationale: Deconditioning with legs unable to support ambulation documented.

note_evidence: Osteoporosis
rationale: Listed in PMH and assessment.

note_evidence: Hyperlipidemia
rationale: Listed in PMH.

note_evidence: Prediabetes
rationale: Listed in PMH.

note_evidence: Significant dental loss — chews carefully on one side, limited to soft foods.
rationale: Tooth loss, one-sided chewing, soft foods documented.

note_evidence: Low back pain radiating to bilateral hips, worse with prolonged standing. Described as a deep, aching, "toothache in the bone" sensation.
rationale: All pain slots documented.

note_evidence: Pain has interfered with sleep on some nights.
rationale: Sleep disturbance from pain documented.

note_evidence: Standing is the primary aggravating factor. Requires support and a target to hold onto when standing. Can turn in bed slowly.
rationale: Standing worsens pain, turns in bed slowly, needs support to stand.

note_evidence: Start **naproxen 220 mg** for baseline aching pain.
rationale: Naproxen 220 mg for baseline pain documented.

note_evidence: Start **meperidine 50 mg** tablets for breakthrough/severe pain (e.g., pre-therapy, severe nighttime pain), used sparingly at the lowest effective dose.
rationale: Meperidine 50 mg PRN severe pain used sparingly documented.

note_evidence: Counseled patient on risks in older adults: drowsiness, cognitive fogginess, unsteadiness, and fall risk. Nursing to monitor closely after each dose.
rationale: Opioid risk counseling and monitoring documented.

note_evidence: Lives alone. No family support nearby; sister died two winters ago. A neighbor assists with taking out bins.
rationale: Isolation, sister's death, neighbor help documented.

note_evidence: Denies any current fear of the former spouse or any other person; states he has been out of her life for years and there is no current safety concern.
rationale: Safety screen negative for current threat documented.

note_evidence: Reports reduced intake over years due to poor dentition and cooking for one; diet often limited to toast and tea.
rationale: Reduced intake due to dentition and cooking for one documented.

note_evidence: **Social work consult** for: (1) discharge support planning, and (2) addressing social isolation — community programs/senior center resources.
rationale: Social work referral for home support and community engagement documented.

note_evidence: **Dietitian consult:** soft/manageable textures with adequate nourishment; emphasis on protein for strength and calcium for bone health; balanced for prediabetes and hyperlipidemia.
rationale: Dietitian referral with specified diet documented.

note_evidence: **Physical therapy:** standing, gait training, lower extremity strengthening.
rationale: PT ordered for standing, walking, strengthening.

note_evidence: **Occupational therapy:** dressing, safe bathing, kitchen/ADL tasks.
rationale: OT ordered for ADLs documented.

note_evidence: Full history and physical examination to be completed at time of admission (as stated during visit).
rationale: History taken but full H&P/physical exam stated as still to be completed.

note_evidence: Complete admission assessment.
rationale: Nursing admission assessment documented in plan.

note_evidence: Team to develop individualized plan of care.
rationale: Individualized plan of care documented.

note_evidence: Every-shift monitoring of pain, blood pressure, skin, and medications.
rationale: Every-shift monitoring of specified parameters documented.

note_evidence: Call button before every stand, at all times including overnight. Nursing fall-risk precautions given osteoporosis.
rationale: Fall precautions and call button counseling due to osteoporosis documented.

note_evidence: **Pre-discharge assessment** required prior to discharge: ability to stand/walk, manage medications and meals, and confirmation of adequate home support.
rationale: Pre-discharge assessment planned with listed components.

note_evidence: Anticipated length of stay approximately two weeks, dependent on strength recovery.
rationale: ~2 week LOS documented.

note_evidence: Patient instructed to report any undesirable cognitive/sedating effects; regimen will be adjusted as needed.
rationale: Counseled to report intolerance with regimen adjustment documented.

note_evidence: following approximately one week of hospitalization... at the time of hospital discharge
rationale: Transfer/discharge from hospital documented.
Note states H&P to be completed later; no finalized H&P note documented.
